description: Generic metadata for racing series identifiers. No trademark content — descriptions only. SPB uses these entries to suggest placement zones and color palettes for series-specific branding.

schema.name: string — generic series descriptor
schema.color_palette: array of hex colors typical of this series style

series.name: Premier Stock Car Series
series.notes: Top-tier stock car series identifier — usually placed prominently in the windshield banner zone. Patriotic palette common.

series.name: Second-Tier Stock Car Series
series.notes: Developmental stock car identifier. Yellow accents are typical of the modern era.

series.name: Pickup Truck Racing Series
series.notes: Truck-bodied stock car series. Use the larger tailgate canvas for series identifier when possible.

series.name: Daytona Prototype Series
series.notes: Top-class American sports car prototype identifier. Small but consistently placed at the splitter leading edge.

series.name: American GT3 Class
series.notes: GT class identifier in American sports car series. Often placed adjacent to the class-color number panel.

series.name: Formula 1 World Championship
series.notes: Premier open-wheel world championship identifier. Tiny but always present at the nose tip.

series.name: American Open-Wheel Series
series.notes: American open-wheel championship identifier. Often placed on the airbox alongside the car number.

series.name: Dirt Late Model Touring Series
series.notes: Touring dirt-oval series identifier. Often hand-applied vinyl with high-contrast palette to survive dust occlusion.

series.name: Outlaw Sprint Car Series
series.notes: Wing-sprint touring series identifier. Top wing has large flat canvas perfect for high-visibility series branding.

series.name: Stock Car Development Series
series.notes: Entry-level stock car development series identifier. Conservative placement matches the series' grassroots feel.

series.name: Professional Drift Series
series.notes: Pro drift series identifier. Bright orange accent ties into the series' high-energy visual identity.

series.name: Global Endurance Championship
series.notes: International endurance championship identifier. Multi-class — placement matches class regulations rather than team preference.
